rulings.Case Title: U.S.BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. JAMES LANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File Amendment To X-Comp.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 15, 2010, line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANTS JAMES LANE, LEONARD KIMARA WOOLFOLK, Motion For Leave To File Amendment To cross-Complaint. Motion denied. Cross-complainant does not explain the delay in seeking the relief and cross-defendant would be prejudiced if the court granted the motion in light of the current trial date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DANILO VARGAS et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 15, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. The City is immune under Government Code sections 845.8(b) and 820.2. Government Code section 845.8 is to be applied broadly. (See Kisbey vs. California (1984) 36 Cal.3rd 415.) A police decision to stop a wrong doer is discretionary. (See Bratt vs. CCSF (1975) 50 Cal.App. 3rd 550.). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES LANIER et al VS. CHIOCK DONG WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Motion To S/A The Settlement And Request A New Trial Date In Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 15, 2010, line 7, PLAINTIFF ANNIE LANIER Amended Motion To Set Aside The Settlement And Request A New Trial Date In Court. Motion denied. Insufficient evidence to support claims of duress or fraud. Stipulation to the agreement was made by the parties personally and in open Court. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA SHEWRY VS. LYUDMILA PASTERNAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination & Reduction Of Lien Pursuant To Welfare & Institution Code Section 14124.76
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 15, 2010, line 21, DEFENDANTS LYDUMILA PASTERNAK, ALBERT STOLL JR. Motion For Determination & Reduction Of Lien Pursuant To Welfare & Institution Code Section 14124.76. Motion for determination and reduction of lien denied as to request to reduce lien amount to zero. Fitch vs. Select Products Company (2005) 36 Cal.4th 812 is not applicable. Fitch held that a Medi-Cal lien may not be asserted in a wrongful death action when the damages recoverable by the plainff in that action do not and could not include compensation for medical services provided to the decedent by Medi-Cal. Here, the underlying case was brought individually and as successor in interest to the decedent. Four of the five causes of action in the first Amended Complaint were brought solely as successor in interest to the decedent with a prayer for damages recoverable under CCP sec. 377.34 including reimbursement for medical expenses. After settlement Ms. Pasternak's counsel initially retained a portion of her recovery to pay the Medi-Cal lien. Ms. Pasternak presents insufficient evidence tht she had "abandoned" all but the wrongful death portion of her claim in the underlying action, and her first-amended complaint and preserving a portion of the settlement to satisfy the lien demonstrate the contrary. Ms. Pasternak and Mr. Stoll have not met the elements of estoppel and there is no showing that this case meets the heightened standard for estoppel against a public agency. The court uses the Ahlborn (2006) 547 U.S. 268 "formula" referenced in W & I 14124.76 based upon a 250,000 claim, which results in the amount owed of $51,728.34. Since the plaintiff is seeking to recover a lesser amount, the court orders the recoverable amount of the lien to be $38,328.81 plus interest as requested. =(302/CWW)
